title: callicarpa subglabra
intro: Callicarpa subglabra, also known as the purple fruit callicarpa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. The plant is native to tropical Asia and is known for its vibrant purple berries, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Callicarpa subglabra include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and providing antioxidant properties, which can be achieved through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
